stem: A factory produces pharmaceutical tablets using two sources. Batch Process Alpha handles 75% of production with a known defect rate of 1.0%. Batch Process Beta handles 25% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 3.8% and 7.7%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected pharmaceutical tablet is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Batch Process Beta?

ground_truth.answer: P(Batch Process Beta | defective) ranges from 0.559 to 0.72 as Batch Process Beta's defect rate varies from 3.8% to 7.7%. Point estimate at 5.7%: 0.655.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.559, 0.72] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
